behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS and MOCK-HIDE both apply: a unit test of mapping logic can pass while real command->artifact-store wiring still writes to the wrong slot. No OS/network/subprocess is required, and no LLM output quality is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage end-to-end against a real local artifact store (e.g., temp DB/filesystem). Assert `grounding` is created/updated with expected payload and `plan` is unchanged by this stage.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module read/write behavior (input artifact selection + output artifact routing). A pure mock can hide miswired artifact keys. No mandatory external infra or real model-quality check is required for this contract.
behaviors.test_description: Seed artifact store with a `grounding` artifact, execute implement-stage flow, then assert: (1) grounding was read (observable via output content or trace metadata), (2) execution output is written to `plan`, and (3) grounding artifact remains intact.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is conditional side-effect suppression across issue-content parser + artifact writer. Unit tests can miss accidental writes caused by orchestration wiring. No real OS/network dependency is necessary.
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content containing pre-existing diagnosis, run deep-dive stage with real local persistence, and assert no write/update occurs to `plan` (idempotent/no-op for that artifact) while expected non-plan outputs/logs still occur.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP is explicit, and correctness depends on cross-step propagation through the full chain. If real LLM-driven stages participate, single-step or mocked tests can miss context-loss/regression that appears only in full sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full chain in order with real stage orchestration and real artifact persistence; verify evaluate produces `grounding`, implement consumes that grounding, and final `plan` contains decisions/constraints traceable to that earlier grounding. Include assertions on artifact lineage across all steps.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: This is a fallback-path state-management check across artifact reads/writes. Mocked dependencies can hide accidental mutation/corruption of sibling artifacts. No external OS/network or model-quality dependency is required.
behaviors.test_description: Start with artifacts that exclude `grounding` but include unrelated artifacts, run implement-stage, then assert: valid `plan` is emitted, no exceptions from missing grounding, and all pre-existing non-plan artifacts remain byte-for-byte unchanged.
